source_name: Code4rena Noya Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: AccountingManager::resetMiddle will not behave as expected
summary: The resetMiddle function in the AccountingManager contract is designed to reset the middle index of either the deposit or withdraw queue. This function is crucial for managing the order and processing of transactions within these queues, particularly in scenarios where recalculations are necessary to ensure fairness and accuracy in the distribution of shares or handling of withdrawals.
root_cause: queue middle-index reset mutates accounting pointers without preserving the intended deposit/withdraw queue ordering semantics
broken_invariants: user exit paths must remain available and value-preserving under the root cause: queue middle-index reset mutates accounting pointers without preserving the intended deposit/withdraw queue ordering semantics
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
reward and distribution accounting must preserve each participant’s accrued value across claims, transfers, and configuration changes
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: The resetMiddle function in the AccountingManager contract is designed to reset the middle index of either the deposit or withdraw queue. This function is crucial for managing the order and processing of transactions within these queues, particularly in scenarios where recalculations are necessary to ensure fairness and accuracy in the distribution of shares or handling of withdrawals.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: To address this issue, the reset function must reset the withdrawal amount requested currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmount (set back to 0), the function should be modified as follows: function resetMiddle( uint256 newMiddle, bool depositOrWithdraw ) public onlyManager { if (depositOrWithdraw) { emit ResetMiddle(newMiddle, depositQueue.middle, depositOrWithdraw);
